summary: Iteration 11 validated the new SecurityHeadersMiddleware + /api/compliance/transport admin endpoint and ran a full regression of prior phases (iter5/6/7/8/9) plus an end-to-end frontend smoke. All 17 iteration_11 tests pass. Full regression passes 102/102 (iter5 20, iter6+7+8 48, iter9 17, iter11 17) when suites are isolated with the known rate-limit-bucket cool-down documented in iteration_10. Frontend loads with CSP active, admin login reaches dashboard, all admin pages render, and zero CSP violations observed across the flow.

minor.issue: Same rate-limit-bucket collision previously documented in iteration_10.json still exists: running iter5 (40-burst login test) immediately before iter6/7/8 trips the per-IP 30/60s bucket on their login fixtures. Workaround: ~80s sleep between suites, or run iter5/iter9 last. Not a product defect.

integration_issues.issue: Sidebar shows 'Audit log' link and backend endpoint is /api/audit-logs, but the React route for the page appears to be /audit (console warning: 'No routes matched location /audit-logs' when probed directly). This is an informational mismatch — the sidebar link itself works fine. Only flagged for consistency.

critical_code_review_comments: SecurityHeadersMiddleware is placed correctly AFTER CORSMiddleware in server.py (line 117), so security headers attach to every outbound response including CORS preflight responses. Verified on /health, authenticated /patients, 401 /auth/me, 404, POST /auth/login. Good.

HSTS is correctly suppressed when APP_ENV is unset (treated as 'dev'). Will only emit on production + https-effective-scheme via x-forwarded-proto. Matches design doc /app/memory/TLS_AND_TRANSPORT_SECURITY.md.

CSP defaults include 'unsafe-inline' in both style-src and script-src. This is documented as an intentional compromise for the CRA-built SPA; before this app is exposed to the public internet, tighten script-src via nonce/sha hashes (CSP_EXTRA is already wired to allow per-env overrides).

setdefault() is used for every header, so downstream middleware or a specific route could override. That's intentional and safe here; just worth flagging so future devs don't accidentally rely on middleware being the only writer.
/api/compliance/transport is admin-gated via require_role('admin') (verified: 401 anon, 403 patient, 200 admin). Response body is HIPAA-safe — no PHI, no secrets; cookie flags are meta-config only.
Nothing in the middleware reads the request body, so it's cheap; dispatch is O(1) header-setting per response. No measurable latency impact observed in the 17 probes.

success_rate.backend: 100% — iter11: 17/17, iter5: 20/20, iter6+7+8: 48/48, iter9: 17/17 (102/102 in isolation). Combined back-to-back run fails ~40 due to the same test-harness ordering rate-limit collision documented in iteration_10; product code is clean.
success_rate.frontend: 100% — login succeeded, admin dashboard rendered, /patients / /compliance / /security / /security-config / /privacy all rendered, zero CSP violations, only expected pre-login 401s on /api/auth/me.

test_credentials: Used /app/memory/test_credentials.md as-is. admin@ccms.app/Admin@ComplianceClinic1 for privileged calls. MFA not enrolled for seeded demo users, so login returned mfa_required=false for all four roles.
seed_data_creation: None. No persistent side-effects beyond a single dev_token password-reset-request against patient@ccms.app (returns token in body in non-prod) and ephemeral rows in login_attempts which are auto-aged.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 11 = TLS/transport security posture. New code: core/security_headers.py (global middleware) + /api/compliance/transport admin endpoint. Verified on live ingress: CSP (default-src 'self', frame-ancestors 'none', upgrade-insecure-requests), X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Permissions-Policy geolocation=() et al, COOP: same-origin, CORP: same-site on every API response — including 200/401/404/POST. HSTS correctly absent (APP_ENV unset => 'dev'). /api/compliance/transport: 401 anon, 403 non-admin, 200 admin with app_env/observed_scheme/cookie_flags/hsts/transport_warnings etc. Prior phase regression confirmed clean in isolation (102/102). NOTE for future runs: the iter5+iter9 40-burst bad-login tests still collide with other suites' login fixtures when run back-to-back through a shared ingress IP; either run them LAST or insert ~80s sleep between suites. That is a harness issue, not a product issue, and is the same issue reported in iteration_10.json.
rca of the issue: No new bugs. Remaining 'issue' is a carry-over test-orchestration artifact: back-to-back pytest runs against the same public ingress IP exhaust the per-IP 30/60s login bucket from the 40-burst bad-login test in iter5/iter9. Mitigation: isolate suites or sleep ~80s between them, as already documented.
